text: For this chapter, you'll need the following supplies. Two bags of organic
mulch, a rake, a hand trowel, a hand cultivator, and a large brown bag.
Before you mulch, weed and cultivate the soil surrounding the tree. Compacted soil puts
extra strain on the tree's root system, so loosening the soil helps roots access water
and oxygen, which is necessary for their survival. Mulch helps to conserve water, control weeds,
and regulate root temperature. It also prevents soil compaction and keeps lawn equipment away
from the base of the tree.
There are basically two types of mulch, organic and inorganic. Organic mulches include wood
chips, pine needles, dried grass clippings, and shredded leaves. At PHS, we recommend
using organic mulch so that your tree has the added benefit of acquiring nutrients as
When mulching your mulch, you should always use a mulch that is not too dry. If you're
mulching young trees, follow the 3-3-3 method. Place the mulch in a three-foot ring around
the base of the tree, three inches deep, and keep the mulch three inches away from the
How often mulch should be reapplied depends on the type of mulch used. Replace wood chips
each spring before weeds start to germinate and spread seeds.
Organic mulches last much longer but do not provide any nutrients to your tree.
